name: @orrgal1/devloop
description: Thin, repo-agnostic dev-loop skills for stacked-PR work. /restack brings a PR's base into its branch (merge by default, --rebase opt-in), syncing the base with upstream first. /restack-all does the same across an entire stack bottom-up. /deslop scans a PR diff and strips AI slop — verbose obvious comments and over-complex local code — reusing the built-in /simplify pass. Infers PR topology from GitHub by default, falls back to raw git analysis. /request-review ranks the most relevant peer reviewer for a PR by signal precedence (same-stack reviewers > reviewers of the author's work > people the author reviews > code-area/CODEOWNERS) and, gated, marks ready + requests them; chain-blind, so forge consumes it via its capability map. /find-blocker classifies whether a PR is held by an external blocker (red/behind base PR, infra incident, sibling PR) across gh/Slack/infra and emits a neutral, waitable condition spec — discovery only, also chain-blind. No dependency on other plugins.
